attacks.name: Sky Legends-GX
attacks.effect: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Fire, Water, and Lightning Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 110 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Each player discards 2 cards from their hand. Your opponent discards first.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Discard up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon that have no damage counters on them and all cards attached to them.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Sky Legends-GX
attacks.effect: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Fire, Water, and Lightning Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 110 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Your opponent reveals their hand. You may choose a Supporter card you find there and use the effect of that card as the effect of this card.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Heal 60 damage and remove all Special Conditions from 1 of your Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Move any number of Water Energy from your Pokémon to your Psyduck, Horsea, Staryu, Starmie-GX, Magikarp, Gyarados, or Lapras in any way you like.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Discard a card from your hand. If you do, look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

fullDisplayName: Misty's Cerulean City Gym 61/68 Hidden Fates
rules:
The attacks of Starmie-GX (both yours and your opponent's) do 40 more damage to the opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

You can play this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.

During this turn, you can play 3 Supporter cards (including this card).

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Each player discards 2 cards from their hand. Your opponent discards first.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Discard up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon that have no damage counters on them and all cards attached to them.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

You can play this card only if you have 4 or fewer other cards in your hand.

Draw a card for each of your opponent's Pokémon in play.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Geodude, Graveler, Golem, Onix-GX, Cubone, Rhyhorn, Rhydon, or Sudowoodo.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

fullDisplayName: Brock's Pewter City Gym 54/68 Hidden Fates
rules:
Onix-GX (both yours and your opponent's) take 40 less damage from the opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Shuffle 6 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.

Draw 2 cards for each Fire Pokémon you have in play.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal up to 2 Trainer cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

flavorText: It doesn't do anything other than eat and sleep. When prompted to make a serious effort, though, it apparently displays awesome power.
Current studies show it can evolve into an incredible eight different species of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Curiosity
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand.

attacks.name: Spin Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The question of why only Eevee has such unstable genes has still not been solved.

attacks.name: Parental Fury
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Kangaskhan protects its child by keeping it in its pouch. It has zero forgiveness for those who harm its child and will beat them down.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It seems that other Pokémon's efforts to take its delicious, nutritious egg away from it caused Chansey to get faster at fleeing.
The plant stalk it holds is its weapon. The stalk is used like a sword to cut all sorts of things.

attacks.name: Sky Legends-GX
attacks.effect: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Fire, Water, and Lightning Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 110 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Happy Mime
attacks.effect: Each player draws a card.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its pantomime skills are wonderful. You may become enraptured while watching it, but next thing you know, Mr. Mime has made a real wall.

attacks.name: Rolling Rush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Lovely Star-GX
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Recordings of Jigglypuff's strange lullabies can be purchased from department stores. These CDs can be found near the bedding area.
It can't help but hear a pin drop from over half a mile away, so it lives deep in the mountains where there aren't many people or Pokémon.
They're popular, but they're rare. Trainers who show them off recklessly may be targeted by thieves.

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They're popular, but they're rare. Trainers who show them off recklessly may be targeted by thieves.

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The skull it wears on its head is that of its dead mother. According to some, it will evolve when it comes to terms with the pain of her death.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Rocky Avalanche-GX
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 3 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.

attacks.name: Mudslide
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Fighting Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.
Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they're totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.
Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psycrush-GX
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: It sways its hips to a rhythm all its own. The precise movements of Jynx living in Alola are truly wonderful.
If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.
Its thin, balloon-like body is inflated by horribly toxic gases. It reeks when it is nearby.

attacks.name: Rocket Tail
attacks.effect: If Jessie & James is in your discard pile, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The latest research has determined that there are over 20 possible arrangements of the patterns on its stomach.
By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

attacks.name: Hurricane Call
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear from clouds while dropping enormous lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its lungs contain an organ that creates electricity. The crackling sound of electricity can be heard when it exhales.

attacks.name: Electroblast
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It explodes in response to even minor stimuli. It is feared, with the nickname of "The Bomb Ball."
It was discovered when Poké Balls were introduced. It is said that there is some connection.

attacks.name: Spark Ball-GX
attacks.effect: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Its nature is to store up electricity. Forests where nests of Pikachu live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning.

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Clean, clear waters are its usual habitat. When it's about to be attacked by an invading enemy, it dives into the water to hide.
It likes swimming around with people on its back. In the Alola region, it's an important means of transportation over water.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam-GX
attacks.effect: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: In the distant past, they were fairly strong, but they have become gradually weaker over time.

attacks.name: Star Stream
attacks.effect: Attach 2 Water Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Numbing Water
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: In many places, there are folktales of stardust falling into the ocean and becoming Staryu.

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Alolan home cooking involves drying Slowpoke tails and then simmering them into a salty stew.
Using psychokinesis gives it a headache, so it normally passes the time spacing out and doing as little as possible.
Its entire body is burning. When it breathes, the temperature rises. When it sneezes, flames shoot out!

attacks.name: Flare Blitz-GX
attacks.effect: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: It lashes about with its tail to knock down its foe. It then tears up the fallen opponent with sharp claws.
The flame on its tail indicates Charmander's life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly.

attacks.name: Guillotine-GX
attacks.effect: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Its two sharp scythes are more than just weapons. It uses them with dexterity to dress its prey before eating.
Whether it's due to a lack of moisture or a lack of nutrients, in Alola the mushrooms on Paras don't grow up quite right.
Its wings are covered in toxic scales. If it finds bird Pokémon going after Caterpie, Butterfree sprinkles its scales on them to drive them off.
Its shell is filled with a thick liquid. All of the cells throughout its body are being rebuilt in preparation for evolution.
Perhaps because it would like to grow up quickly, it has a voracious appetite, eating a hundred leaves a day.
